title: elymus dahuricus subsp. micranthus
intro: Elymus dahuricus subsp. micranthus, commonly known as a type of wild grass, is a plant species that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Elymus dahuricus subsp. micranthus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat various ailments and maintain overall well-being.
